summary:
Bubble CPAP produces an audible bubbling sound from gas flowing through water, while high-flow nasal cannulae deliver heated, humidified air silently without liquid components. Because HFNC lacks this source of noise, it is not noisier than bubble CPAP; hence, the correct answer is B.  

\boxed{B}
formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): Bubble CPAP produces an audible bubbling sound from gas flowing through water, while high-flow nasal cannulae deliver heated, humidified air silently without liquid components. Because HFNC lacks this source of noise, it is not noisier than bubble CPAP; hence, the correct answer is B.  

\boxed{B}. Answer choice: B
